discussion_posts.name: imsure
discussion_posts.content: There is one `ambiguity` of the question: by looking at the official solution, there is an `unspecified assumption` for this problem: the words are matched only at the same position, e.g., `words1[0]` is matched against `words2[0]`, `words1[1]` is matched against `words2[1]`, so on and so forth. However, considering this two sentences: `Bob is a talented actor` and `a talented actor is Bob`, shouldn't they also be similar? But the official solution gave `false` in this case.

discussion_posts.name: mcgga
discussion_posts.content: Interesting problem except that the description is long. \nShould be a `Medium` one as there are some implicit conditions to consider and a `hash table in hash table` to be constructed.

discussion_posts.name: Satansoft
discussion_posts.content: **Easy**, but same word could be similar to different words, so pairing like `[x, y]` and `[y, x]` are not enough, but `[x, [a, b, c, ...]]` and `[y,  [x, y , z, ...]]` works well.

This is a very typcial question where the discription make the question seems like a hard one. 

Check same index in each sentence.

If they are in the pair set you you will have a True pair, if not then directly return False. 

checking all the pair, the return the truth value. 
and make a check if the elemenet is the same or not
